sense.serverInstructions:
When Sense is available and indexed, prefer Sense tools over grep, glob, and file-walking agents for structural and semantic code questions. Sense provides pre-indexed results that are faster and more complete.

WHEN TO USE SENSE TOOLS:
- Symbol relationships, callers, dependencies → sense_graph
- Who holds/embeds X, which structs carry it → sense_graph (composed_by)
- What retains X transitively, incl. behind interface fields (lifecycle audit) → sense_blast (retained_via_interfaces)
- "What would break if I changed X?", impact analysis → sense_blast
- Conceptual/semantic code search (not exact string match) → sense_search
- Project patterns and conventions → sense_conventions
- Index health, what's indexed → sense_status

WORKFLOWS:
- Orientation (new to the codebase?) → sense_search with broad concepts + sense_conventions
- Impact analysis (changing something?) → sense_blast
- Dependency tracing (who calls what?) → sense_graph
- Debugging (where does X happen?) → sense_search
- Refactoring (what patterns exist?) → sense_conventions + sense_graph

WHEN NOT TO USE SENSE TOOLS:
- Exact text/string search → use grep
- Reading file contents → use your file reading tool
- Editing code → Sense is read-only
